ref: Then after her baby is born, name him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.”
text: Abalofo osuɔtɔbi, faatika nwɔ leyooto falɛ Yesu, diekye nnwɔɔ nimadiki batii nɔɔ ankyeko sikpile lɛma kamɛ nɛ.”
translated: Some people, when they hear the word about Jesus, they receive it with joy but they don't have deep roots so they fall away.

ref: “In Ramah a voice was heard crying and weeping loudly. Rachel was mourning for her children, and she refused to be comforted, because they were dead.” The Return from Egypt
text: “Batii lanu sɛlɔ sewo di Rama alɛ boowi kaku osie kanya ku sɛnyaami. Rahel nikowi anta babi nɔɔ. Eesĩ kafɔɔ alɛ batanlo sɔɔ, diekye babi nɔɔ nwu nkpo lekpi.” Yosef, Maria Ku Yesu Lakple Di Egipte Kasɔ Suoto.
translated: After they left, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, "Get up, take the child and his mother and escape to Egypt. Stay there until I tell you, for Herod is going to search for the child to kill him." So Joseph got up, took the child and his mother during the night and left for Egypt.

ref: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
text: Ni aatɔkɔ nwɔ alɛ, “Taka, faakpaa obisɔ nwu nkpo ku ɔya biekple bisifi Israel kasɔ suoto. Diekye batii ba nikomiɛ alɛ balo ni obisɔ nwu nkpo ntookpi.”
translated: And God said to him, "Take all the leaders of the people and hang them before the Lord in broad daylight, so that the fierce anger of the Lord may turn away from Israel."

ref: and they went to live there in the town of Nazareth. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “He will be called a Nazarene.”
text: Obe wɔ aalaabuo ni nfa, aasifi aalaasiɛ di okpoo wɔ bɔɔlɛɛ ni Nasaret. Nfa lɛma ɔlaasiɛ lata alɛ, ɔlaa wɔ di Yaa ɔlaa babuɛtɛtɛ labuɛ baatɛ ni alɛ, “Batii malɛɛ nwɔ ni Nasaret otii yi,” diawa kaanya ninkpo.
translated: When the days of purification were completed, they brought him to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord. As the law of Moses commanded them, saying, "Every firstborn male shall be dedicated to the Lord," as it is written in the law.

ref: He said, “Turn back to God! The kingdom of heaven will soon be here.”
text: aatoobuɛ alɛ, “Bidiɛ sikpile ɔbla, diekye Yaa sɛka kalekɔ ntɔɔtɛɛtɛɛ.”
translated: "The car said, "When the road is slippery, then Yaa drives very carefully."

ref: When Jesus heard that John had been put in prison, he went to Galilee.
text: Di obe wɔ di Yesu lanu alɛ batoomufũ Yohane bɛɛtɛ ni di leyo katenkɔ yi, ni aasifi Galilea nɛ.
translated: When Jesus heard that John the Baptist had been arrested, he left for Galilee.

ref: Then the devil came to him and said, “If you are God's Son, tell these stones to turn into bread.”
text: Ninfɛ di Abonsam lakyɛ ɔkyɛ nɔɔ aalaatɔkɔ nwɔ alɛ, “Nse Yaa obi fale, ni tɔkɔ afuɔ ya mmle falɛ akple alesaa.”
translated: "When Abonsam's children were going to the farm, they said, 'If Yaa comes, tell her we have gone to the farm and will return in the evening.'"

ref: You are the salt for everyone on earth. But if salt no longer tastes like salt, how can it make food salty? All it is good for is to be thrown out and walked on.
text: “Ote bite fɛ mba biɛta batii bamuu. Kafɔɔ nse mba nwu nkpo ɔmɛnɛfɛ nɔɔ ntooloo, lɛsalɛsaa kuninwii nsinnaa babayɔ mankpee mi ni ɔmɛnɛfɛ. Disinnaa nfasũ kunnwii, nioso ɔyɔ man-yɔ manwii batii nnanafu mi manfe.
translated: We should help those people who are suffering. Because when we see someone in difficulty or trouble, especially children or elderly people with problems. When we help them, God will also help us when we are in need.

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
text: “Bitoonu kafɔɔ alɛ, baatɔkɔ bakofo alɛ, ‘Tansĩ ntam be fɛɛka ni ɔbla. Kafɔɔ bibla di lɛsaa le suooto biɛka ntam biɛta ni Saate nwu.’
translated: "The elders said, the ancestors said, 'Long ago there was a famine in the land. The elders gathered together and went to consult the oracle about what they should do to end the famine.'"

ref: When you make a promise, say only “Yes” or “No.” Anything else comes from the devil. Revenge (Luke
text: Bita yii lee dibla yii, biɛta oowo lee dibla oowo. Nle mmle kamaa, lɛsaa lele biekple biebuɛ kyiditɛ nwu nfũ diekye. Yesu Latuo Asaa Alɛ Butambla Okpile Buakyisa.
translated: One person's food is another person's poison. When we say this, it means that what is good for one person may be bad for another person. Jesus Christ the Son of God Died on the Cross for Our Sins.

ref: Then you will be acting like your Father in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both good and bad people. And he sends rain for the ones who do right and for the ones who do wrong.
text: Ni bibakple Teeye wɔ ninkpe ni kaatoo babi. Diekye nnwɔɔ ninta kufĩ nɔɔ mpɛ kunta batii kpile ku babienetɛ kyɛkyɛ. Anta kafɔɔ kanto nɔɔ nnɔɔ anta batii ba ninte ni tinini ku mba ninante ni tinini kyɛkyɛ.
translated: We thank God for giving us this day. May we all live in peace and love one another in our community. So that we may see tomorrow and our children may also see tomorrow in peace.

ref: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
text: “Nse biɔpɛ ɔlaa minta Yaa yi, bitambla fɛ mmle baatii ba nimbla ni asaa di anu anu mbla nii. Mambɔmbɔ ɔtaka oyila di Yaa batii kasiisakɔ ku akpaka alɛ batii bamuu kanya ma alɛ bɔɔpɛ ɔlaa manta. Kɔtɔkɔ ye nlɛ batoofũ letota lɛma kofokofoko.
translated: When we call upon the name of God, we must do so with a pure heart and sincere intentions. Sometimes the path that God shows us may seem difficult or different from what we expected. But we must trust in His guidance completely.

ref: When you pray, go into a room alone and close the door. Pray to your Father in private. He knows what is done in private and will reward you.
text: Nioso nse fɔɔpɛ ɔlaa fanta Yaa yi, faabuo leyo tenle lɛfɔ kamɛ, faasɛɛ, faapɛ ɔlaa fata Teefɔ wɔ manannya ni. Ni Teefɔ wɔ ninnya ni lɛsaa di kawofakɔ yi, abatafɔ letota biene.
translated: When a person does something bad to you, do not think about revenge, instead, do something good to that person so that they will be ashamed. If that person is ashamed and changes their ways, you have gained a friend.

ref: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
text: Sɛka kalekɔ lɛfɔ diwa wo kaamɛ. Ta babla fɔ dibiesaa kaasɔ ka mmle suoto fɛ mmle dikpe ni di osi.
translated: The dog ran into the forest yesterday. It chased a rabbit through the thick bushes and tall grass.

ref: Then others won't know you are going without eating. But your Father sees what is done in private, and he will reward you. Treasures in Heaven (Luke
text: mmle okle di batii dilabatofo alɛ bionii ni kanya. Diediki Teeye wɔ manannya ni lete nikatofo. Ni Teeye wɔ ninnya ni asaa ya biɔbla ni di kawofakɔ mata ye letota biene. Yesu Latuo Asaa Di Yaa Kafa Lɛsaawɛ Suoto.
translated: We should not forget that God loves us. Because God is good and merciful to us. And God wants us to repent and turn away from our evil ways and come to him. Jesus Christ is the Way, the Truth, and the Life.

ref: But more than anything else, put God's work first and do what he wants. Then the other things will be yours as well.
text: Kafɔɔ bita anu asɛɛ ye di Yaa Kafa Sɛka ɔkpɛ suoto alenkee di asaa amuu ɔbla kamɛ, biɛbla nwɔ dibiesaa. Ni Yaa mayɔ asaa amuu bule ankyakaa ye.
translated: Long ago when God created the world and put people on earth to live, he did not create death. And God did not want people to die at all.

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: Kafɔɔ kɔtɔkɔ ye nlɛ, bia Ɔka Salomo omu, di lɛsaawɛ nɔɔ nimuu kamɛ, atakpee awu alɛ onyu aawo lepoo kamɛ atoto kpeele ya mmle kaamɛ lɛma onwii.
translated: When the Queen of Sheba heard about Solomon's fame, she came to test him with difficult questions, and she arrived with a very large caravan with camels carrying spices and gold and precious stones.

ref: Treat others as you want them to treat you. This is what the Law and the Prophets are all about. The Narrow Gate (Luke
text: Asaa ya biomiɛ bilɛ batii bamba babla bata ye ni yi, aye bibla nkpo okle bita ma. Nle mmle ninle Mose kufiofa ku Yaa ɔlaa babuɛtɛtɛ asaa ya bootuo ni kasɔ nɛ. Bife Di Kayoko Ka Ninle Ni Kabiibii Kamɛ Bibuo.
translated: When people do things that are not good, they bring problems upon themselves. That is why Moses warned the Israelites to be careful. God's Word Helps Us to Make Good Decisions.

ref: He taught them like someone with authority, and not like their teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: Asaa nɔɔ otuo ditate fɛ mmle okle di kufiofa batuotɛ ntuo nii. Kafɔɔ aatuo asaa ku sɛkambi.
translated: The man went to the farm to work but he did not have a cutlass to work with. So the man returned home.

ref: so the disciples went over to him and woke him up. They said, “Lord, wake up! Save us before we drown!”
text: Basaateketetɛ nwu nkpo lakyɛ baalaasɛnkɛsa nwɔ, ni baatɔkɔ nwɔ alɛ, “Saate, diki wo! Nfɛ buookpi lo!”
translated: They woke him up and said to him, "Master, save us! We are perishing!"

ref: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.

translated: The bird flew over the house and landed on the tree.

ref: The people taking care of the pigs ran to the town and told everything, especially what had happened to the two men.
text: Nioso aplakuo nwu bakpatɛ latoso baasifi okpoo baalaabuɛ lɛsaa le niawa ni di basuɔtɔ ba kamɛ di anwuna kpile nwu lasiɛ ni ku aplakuo nwu suoto baatɔkɔ batii.
translated: When a person dies, their relatives gather together and mourn them for seven days, and after that the person is buried and they perform funeral rites.

text: Kafɔɔ baadie ni ninfa yi, baasifi baalaayie ɔlaa wɔ mmle lɛkya di Yesu suoto baakyakasa ntɛɛ nwu nkpo kasɔ fɛɛ. Yesu Layɔɔsa Osuɔtɔ Onwii Nintatoofuo Ni Ɔkakatɛ Ofiɛ.
translated: After his resurrection, his disciples were convinced that Jesus had truly risen from the dead. Jesus the Savior Gives His Disciples Power and Sends Them Out.

ref: But when someone arrests you, don't worry about what you will say or how you will say it. At that time you will be given the words to say.

translated: When a person becomes a Christian, they must put away old things and forget them, not remember old things again. When someone does this, God will give them new things.

ref: But you will not really be the one speaking. The Spirit from your Father will tell you what to say.

translated: We should not forget God's words. We should always remember God's holy words in our hearts.

ref: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.

translated: The big bird flew away to its nest. When the bird returned in the evening it brought food, God's work is wonderful indeed.

ref: If you tell others you belong to me, I will tell my Father in heaven you are my followers.

translated: When they saw that we were not afraid of them, they also were not afraid to come to Teemi and Yaa's house where we were.

ref: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.

translated: The child is eating food that his mother cooked. Wow! The child is eating food. The food is very sweet.

ref: If you love your father or mother or even your sons and daughters more than me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
text: “Otii lele niɛbɔmbɔ ote ee ɔya eelenkee mi yi, alaafuo mi saateketetɛ ole. Olele kafɔɔ niɛbɔmbɔ obi suɔtɔbi ee obi sankobi eelenkee mi yi, aasiɛko alɛ aale mi saateketetɛ.
translated: When someone does something bad to you, you should forgive them. Even if someone does something bad to you seven times, you should still forgive them.

ref: And anyone who gives one of my most humble followers a cup of cool water, just because that person is my follower, will be rewarded.
text: Bitofo nle mmle nwaa bilɛ, otii lele niekusii ntu yɔɔle kakɔkɔɛ kanwii ɛɛta batikankotɛ nii ba mmle ɔyɛntɛle kaamɛ lɛma ta ami oso, Yaa dilabasĩ nwɔ letota biene ɔta.”
translated: When you go to visit someone, if you see that the person is not in a good mood or is busy with something important, don't stay long, say what you came to say and leave immediately.

ref: to ask Jesus, “Are you the one we should be looking for? Or must we wait for someone else?”
text: Ni balaakaalɛ nwɔ alɛ, “Afɔ ninle otii wɔ di Yaa labuɛ aatɛ alɛ fabawa ni ɛɛ, sɛɛ buatoonyu osuku munta otii bamba?”
translated: And they said to him, "Why do the disciples of John and the disciples of the Pharisees fast, but your disciples do not fast?"

ref: People of Capernaum, do you think you will be honored in heaven? You will go down to hell! If the miracles that took place in your town had happened in Sodom, it would still be standing.

translated: And you Capernaum, will you be lifted up to heaven? No! You will go down to hell. Because if the miracles that were done in you had been done in Sodom, it would have remained until today.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he said to them: Any kingdom where people fight each other will end up ruined. And a town or family that fights will soon destroy itself.
text: Yesu latofo disibu lɛma nwu, ni aatɔkɔ ma alɛ, “Sɛka kalekɔ lele niɛsɛ bawo di akudi akudi bɛɛtaka bayɛ baawo suoto yi, manyila. Nkpo okle kafɔɔ se okpoo ee leyo lewo kamɛ batii lɛsɛ bawo akudi akudi bɔɔkpɛko bawo yi, mambiɛ.
translated: Jesus answered them and said, "Truly, truly I say to you, you seek me not because you saw signs, but because you ate of the loaves and were filled. Do not work for the food that perishes, but for the food that endures to eternal life, which the Son of Man will give to you."

ref: On the day of judgment the people of Nineveh will stand there with you and condemn you. They turned to God when Jonah preached, and yet here is something far greater than Jonah.
text: Di lɛkankoyi nwu suoto yi, Niniwe batii mataka manyila makanko ye malo ye lɛpɔɔ, diekye baakyi di sikpile lɛma suoto di obe wɔ baanu ni Yona ɔlaa obuɛ. Kɔtɔkɔ ye nlɛ otii onwii nkpe ninfũ ɛɛtaka katoo alenke ni Yona.
translated: In this lesson, we will see how the people of Nineveh responded to the message that God gave, and also we will learn some lessons from the book of Jonah. Therefore, let us carefully examine what happened to Jonah.

ref: While Jesus was still speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers came and stood outside because they wanted to talk with him.
text: Di obe wɔ di Yesu latɔɔkakatɛko ni batii nwu nkpo yi, Yesu ɔya ku Yesu basuɔtɔbi lɛma lawa nfa. Baabayila kaayi alɛ boomiɛ alɛ bakakatɛko nwɔ.
translated: When they saw the miracles that Jesus was doing, Jesus went and Jesus' disciples followed him. They went to a place where many people gathered.

ref: Many prophets and good people were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear. Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Mark
text: Diekye nwaako kɔtɔkɔ ye nlɛ, Yaa ɔlaa babuɛtɛtɛ kpinwu ku batii ba ninte ni tinini lamiɛ osie kanya alɛ bɔɔnya lɛsaa le biɔnya nii, kafɔɔ batanya. Baamiɛ kafɔɔ osie kanya alɛ boonu lɛsaa le bionu nii, kafɔɔ batanu.” Yesu Ladiki Lɛkpa Nwu Nkpo Kasɔ Di Alesaa Kubi Nwu Santɛ Suoto.
translated: Because of this, God gave them over to shameful lusts. Even their women exchanged natural sexual relations for unnatural ones. Men committed shameful acts with other men, and received in themselves the due penalty for their error." Jesus Christ Is The Only Way To Eternal Life In Heaven.

ref: The seeds that fell on good ground are the people who hear and understand the message. They produce as much as
text: Alesaa kubi ko kafɔɔ niakpite ni di kasɔ nwaa suoto ninle batii ba nienu ni ɔlaa nwu beenu ni kasɔ. Beetika abi, bawo kɔlafa, bawo afosi akuɔ, bawo kafɔɔ afosi atiɛ.” Yesu Lapɛ Lɛkpa Aakyeko Sɛfa Suoto.
translated: Therefore, when you see the abomination of desolation standing in the holy place, as spoken of by Daniel the prophet, let the reader understand, then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains, let him who is on the housetop not go down to take anything out of his house, and let him who is in the field not turn back to take his cloak." Jesus Speaks About the Great Tribulation.

ref: Jesus replied, “They don't have to leave. Why don't you give them something to eat?”
text: Yesu ladiki kanya alɛ, “Diefĩ alɛ baasifi. Aye bamu bimiɛ lɛsaa lewo bita ma bale.”
translated: "Jesus said to them, "The harvest is plentiful, but the workers are few.""

ref: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into a boat and start back across the lake. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
text: Ninfɛ di Yesu lata alɛ basaateketetɛ nɔɔ layie baabuo di oklo kamɛ alɛ balekatũ bataalɛ basifi lekpo nwu nkpo kakyɛ nyɔɔfa nɛ. Diabu lete nɔɔ ninfa alɛ oodiki batii nwu nkpo osuku.
translated: When Jesus' disciples saw that the people were hungry and there was no food to give them, they were very worried. But Jesus showed them that he could perform miracles.

ref: When they saw him, they thought he was a ghost. They were terrified and started screaming.
text: Di obe wɔ kamɛ baanyɔɔ akyɛ di ntu osi ɔɔwa nii, lɛyɛkɛ lapɛ ma baabuɛ alɛ, “Kpi dile!” Nkpo oso baayiisa kudu osie kanya!
translated: When they saw the great fish coming towards their boat, the fisherman shouted to them, "Paddle faster!" But they were already very tired!

ref: Jesus answered: Why do you disobey God and follow your own teaching?
text: Ninfɛ di Yesu ladiki kanya alɛ, “Be oso di aye kafɔɔ biesĩ Yaa kufiofa ole, bitikanko saa bakofo loo ɔsaabla nkpaamaa?
translated: The disciples of Jesus asked him, "Why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?"

ref: Stay away from those Pharisees! They are like blind people leading other blind people, and all of them will fall into a ditch.”
text: Nkpo oso bitankpee ma kanya. Banumbiɛtɛ bale bɔɔkpaa banumbiɛtɛ wo lɛma. Nse numbiɛtɛ kafɔɔ kɔkpaa numbiɛtɛ wo nɔɔ yi, akyuu lɛma anyɔ, babanɔɔ mambuo diibiɔ kamɛ.”
translated: One thing I want you to know. The believers who have died are with the believers who are alive. When a believer dies and joins the believers who have gone before, there is no separation, they are together in one place.

ref: Then his disciples came over to him and asked, “Do you know you insulted the Pharisees by what you said?”
text: Ni baasaateketetɛ nwu nkpo lawa ɔkyɛ nɔɔ babatɔkɔ nwɔ alɛ, “Faye falɛ sɛlaa se feebuɛ ni lebuo Farisi batii nwu nkpo suoto osi kanya?”
translated: His disciples came and asked him, "Why do you speak to them in parables, but you teach us the law of the Pharisees openly?"

ref: Don't you know that the food you put into your mouth goes into your stomach and then out of your body?
text: Biye bilɛ lɛsaa lele di otii lekpee kaanya kamɛ, kafutu kamɛ ninsifi, nni kamaa, ankyɛ ni an-yu.
translated: A child who does not listen to advice will experience hardship, suffer poverty, face hunger, and also shame.

ref: Jesus left and went to the territory near the towns of Tyre and Sidon.
text: Yesu ladie ninfa, ni aasifi ntɛɛ be niɛtɛɛtɛɛ ni sekpoo kplɛ se ninle ni Tiro ku Sidon nɛ.
translated: Jesus left there, and went away to the region of Tyre and Sidon.

ref: Jesus called his disciples together and told them, “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat. I don't want to send them away hungry. They might faint on their way home.”
text: Yesu lalɛɛ basaateketetɛ nɔɔ aawako ɔkyɛ nɔɔ, ni aatɔkɔ ma alɛ, “Batii ba mmle nwu nkpo ɔlaa konu mi kafutu, diekye batɔɔwɛ di ɔkyɛ nii ayi atiɛ amu kafɔɔ lɛsalɛsaa nnaa ma baale nii. Noomiɛ nlɛ ndiki ma osuku baatɔɔkyɛ leyo ku kɔka. Nse diele nkpo, babalaatokpi kɔka di osuku.”
translated: Jesus saw the tax collectors and sinners coming to him, and he said to them, "Those who are well do not need a doctor to treat them, because they are not sick and do not need medicine. But it is the sick person who needs to go and find a doctor. If it were not so, they would not be looking for a doctor at all."

ref: Jesus asked them how much food they had. They replied, “Seven small loaves of bread and a few little fish.”
text: Ni di Yesu lakaalɛ ma alɛ, “Bloblo akudi asɛ bikle?” Ni baasaateketetɛ nwu latɔkɔ nwɔ alɛ, “Bloblo akudi akuɛnsĩ ku kɔkpaku biibii kɔsɛɛ kowo ko bukle.”
translated: And Jesus asked them, "How many loaves do you have?" And they answered him saying, "Seven loaves and a few small fish."

ref: and said, “Lord, have pity on my son! He has a bad case of epilepsy and often falls into a fire or into water.
text: Ni aatɔkɔ Yesu alɛ, “Saate, nya obi nii suɔtɔbi wɔ mmle nyaami. Kakpibubu nsi nwɔ kɔɔnwɔtɔ nwɔ osie kanya. Obe lele ɔɔnɔɔ ambuo di ntu ku ɔtɔ kamɛ.
translated: Then Jesus said, "Satan, go away from me, because it is written that you shall worship the Lord your God and serve Him only."

ref: Then Jesus spoke sternly to the demon. It went out of the boy, and right then he was healed.
text: Ninfɛ di Yesu lasĩ ɔlaa aata ninwuna kpile le niasiɛ ni di obisɔ nwu nkpo kamɛ, ninfɛ diadie nɛ. Nfanwu ni di ofiɛ nwu nkpo layɔɔ nwɔ nɛ.
translated: When Jesus' disciples saw that they had only a little food left and were hungry, they were worried. But they did not know what to do about it.

translated: After they left, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream saying, "Get up, take the child and his mother and flee to Egypt, and remain there until I tell you, for Herod is about to search for the child to destroy him." ref: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
text: Ni aatɔkɔ nwɔ alɛ, “Taka, faakpaa obisɔ nwu nkpo ku ɔya biekple bisifi Israel kasɔ suoto. Diekye batii ba nikomiɛ alɛ balo ni obisɔ nwu nkpo ntookpi.”
translated: "And God said to him, "Take all these things and divide them among the children of Israel as an inheritance. Therefore they called that place the well of the oath."

ref: and they went to live there in the town of Nazareth. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “He will be called a Nazarene.”
text: Obe wɔ aalaabuo ni nfa, aasifi aalaasiɛ di okpoo wɔ bɔɔlɛɛ ni Nasaret. Nfa lɛma ɔlaasiɛ lata alɛ, ɔlaa wɔ di Yaa ɔlaa babuɛtɛtɛ labuɛ baatɛ ni alɛ, “Batii malɛɛ nwɔ ni Nasaret otii yi,” diawa kaanya ninkpo.
translated: When the days of purification were completed, they brought him to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord. As the law of Moses required, they said, "Every firstborn male shall be consecrated to the Lord," as it is written in the law.

ref: He said, “Turn back to God! The kingdom of heaven will soon be here.”
text: aatoobuɛ alɛ, “Bidiɛ sikpile ɔbla, diekye Yaa sɛka kalekɔ ntɔɔtɛɛtɛɛ.”
translated: "The driver said, "When the child was crying, then Yaa's mother was laughing loudly."

ref: When Jesus heard that John had been put in prison, he went to Galilee.
text: Di obe wɔ di Yesu lanu alɛ batoomufũ Yohane bɛɛtɛ ni di leyo katenkɔ yi, ni aasifi Galilea nɛ.
translated: When Jesus called the apostle John and his brother to follow him, they left Galilee.

ref: Then the devil came to him and said, “If you are God's Son, tell these stones to turn into bread.”
text: Ninfɛ di Abonsam lakyɛ ɔkyɛ nɔɔ aalaatɔkɔ nwɔ alɛ, “Nse Yaa obi fale, ni tɔkɔ afuɔ ya mmle falɛ akple alesaa.”
translated: "When Abonsam's wife saw that her husband had not returned home, she said, 'Something has happened to Yaa, and her husband has also gone to look for her.'"

ref: Finally, the devil took Jesus up on a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms on earth and their power.
text: Ninfɛ di Abonsam nwu nkpo lakple aakpaa Yesu aasifiko kobokote takale kunwii nsintɛɛ. Ninfɛ aadiki kayi ka mmle sɛka nlekɔ mmuu ku sɛlɛ ninya aatuo nwɔ nɛ.
translated: When the demons saw that Jesus had come to their area, they were very afraid. They knew that he had power to drive them out of that place.

ref: News about him spread all over Syria, and people with every kind of sickness or disease were brought to him. Some of them had a lot of demons in them, others were thought to be crazy, and still others could not walk. But Jesus healed them all.
text: Leyooto nɔɔ lakyaka Siria kasɔ kamuu suoto. Nkpo oso batii lakpaa batii ba bamuu nikofiɛ ni sifiɛ ate ate baawako ɔkyɛ nɔɔ monoofuo ni lele kuniwii ɔbla. Batii ba di anwuna kpile nisi ni kaamɛ ɔɔkɔkpɛko ma nii, mba nikofiɛ ni kakpibubu, ku mba niekpi ni kakyɛkakyɛ. Ni di Yesu layɔɔsa bamuu lɛma ofiɛ nɛ.
translated: People brought to Jesus a paralyzed man from Syria who was very sick. Some people carried him on a mat because he could not walk on his own and they brought him to where Jesus was teaching and healing people. They brought him because they believed that Jesus could heal him, and also because of their faith and their love. When they came to Jesus, they asked him to heal the man.

ref: God blesses those people who are humble. The earth will belong to them!
text: Suoto siyɔɔ batii ba niɛwakosa ni suoto kasɔ, diekye mma nimale kayi ka mmle asaa nɛ.
translated: When a person is sick and goes to the hospital, they give him medicine to drink so he can get well.

ref: God blesses those people whose hearts are pure. They will see him!
text: Suoto siyɔɔ batii ba siitu lɛma ntika ni lɛɛba ninwii, diekye mma nimanya Yaa nɛ.
translated: Therefore we should not think that the divine being is like gold or silver or stone, an image formed by the art and imagination of man.

ref: You are the salt for everyone on earth. But if salt no longer tastes like salt, how can it make food salty? All it is good for is to be thrown out and walked on.
text: “Ote bite fɛ mba biɛta batii bamuu. Kafɔɔ nse mba nwu nkpo ɔmɛnɛfɛ nɔɔ ntooloo, lɛsalɛsaa kuninwii nsinnaa babayɔ mankpee mi ni ɔmɛnɛfɛ. Disinnaa nfasũ kunnwii, nioso ɔyɔ man-yɔ manwii batii nnanafu mi manfe.
translated: We should help those people who are suffering. Because when we see someone in difficulty or trouble, especially children and elderly people with problems. When we help them, God also blesses us with good things.

ref: Don't suppose I came to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I did not come to do away with them, but to give them their full meaning.
text: Yesu lakple aabuɛ alɛ, “Bitannyu bilɛ ɔwa lɛwa nlɛ kɔmaklee Mose kufiofa ku Yaa ɔlaa babuɛtɛtɛ asaa otuo ndiki. Ninwa nlɛ kɔmaklee nya ndiki. Kafɔɔ ɔwa lɛwa nlɛ asaa ya beetuo ni kawa kaanya.
translated: Jesus answered them, "The children of this age marry and are given in marriage according to the law of Moses and what God commanded them to do. But those who are worthy of that age and the resurrection from the dead neither marry nor are given in marriage.

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
text: “Bitoonu kafɔɔ alɛ, baatɔkɔ bakofo alɛ, ‘Tansĩ ntam be fɛɛka ni ɔbla. Kafɔɔ bibla di lɛsaa le suooto biɛka ntam biɛta ni Saate nwu.’
translated: "The elders said, the leaders said, 'We will not give our daughters in marriage to them. The elders have sworn an oath that we will not give them our daughters to marry.'"

ref: When you make a promise, say only “Yes” or “No.” Anything else comes from the devil. Revenge (Luke
text: Bita yii lee dibla yii, biɛta oowo lee dibla oowo. Nle mmle kamaa, lɛsaa lele biekple biebuɛ kyiditɛ nwu nfũ diekye. Yesu Latuo Asaa Alɛ Butambla Okpile Buakyisa.
translated: One person's food is another person's poison. This means that what one person likes, another person may not like. Jesus Christ the Son of God Came to Save Sinners.

ref: Then you will be acting like your Father in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both good and bad people. And he sends rain for the ones who do right and for the ones who do wrong.
text: Ni bibakple Teeye wɔ ninkpe ni kaatoo babi. Diekye nnwɔɔ ninta kufĩ nɔɔ mpɛ kunta batii kpile ku babienetɛ kyɛkyɛ. Anta kafɔɔ kanto nɔɔ nnɔɔ anta batii ba ninte ni tinini ku mba ninante ni tinini kyɛkyɛ.
translated: We thank God for giving us this day. May we use this opportunity to learn new things and to understand each other well. So that we may know how to live with others and how to relate with others well.

ref: When you do good deeds, don't try to show off. If you do, you won't get a reward from your Father in heaven.
text: “Bilolaa binyu nwaa alɛ biɛbla sifutulɛ lee di lɛkpaka alɛ batii kanya. Nse biɛbla asaa ya mmle di lɛkpaka yi, ni letota kuninwii nnaa ye di Teeye nfũ di Yaa Kafa.
translated: The people who live in the world should prepare themselves for the coming of the Lord. When they prepare their hearts for His coming, they will receive blessings from God our Father and Lord Jesus.

ref: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
text: “Nse biɔpɛ ɔlaa minta Yaa yi, bitambla fɛ mmle baatii ba nimbla ni asaa di anu anu mbla nii. Mambɔmbɔ ɔtaka oyila di Yaa batii kasiisakɔ ku akpaka alɛ batii bamuu kanya ma alɛ bɔɔpɛ ɔlaa manta. Kɔtɔkɔ ye nlɛ batoofũ letota lɛma kofokofoko.
translated: When we serve God, we should do so with all our heart and with complete dedication. Sometimes God tests us to see if we truly love him or if we are just pretending. That is why we must be faithful always.

ref: When you pray, go into a room alone and close the door. Pray to your Father in private. He knows what is done in private and will reward you.
text: Nioso nse fɔɔpɛ ɔlaa fanta Yaa yi, faabuo leyo tenle lɛfɔ kamɛ, faasɛɛ, faapɛ ɔlaa fata Teefɔ wɔ manannya ni. Ni Teefɔ wɔ ninnya ni lɛsaa di kawofakɔ yi, abatafɔ letota biene.
translated: When a person becomes a Christian, he must leave his old ways, that is, he must become a new person in God. When God makes him new, he receives two blessings.

ref: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
text: Sɛka kalekɔ lɛfɔ diwa wo kaamɛ. Ta babla fɔ dibiesaa kaasɔ ka mmle suoto fɛ mmle dikpe ni di osi.
translated: The dog ran into the house yesterday. It was chasing a small bird that flew through the window into the room.

ref: Then others won't know you are going without eating. But your Father sees what is done in private, and he will reward you. Treasures in Heaven (Luke
text: mmle okle di batii dilabatofo alɛ bionii ni kanya. Diediki Teeye wɔ manannya ni lete nikatofo. Ni Teeye wɔ ninnya ni asaa ya biɔbla ni di kawofakɔ mata ye letota biene. Yesu Latuo Asaa Di Yaa Kafa Lɛsaawɛ Suoto.
translated: We should not be afraid of those who persecute us because of our faith. The Bible says that we will receive a reward. The Bible also says that we should rejoice and be glad because our reward in heaven is great. Jesus Christ Is the Way, the Truth, and the Life.

ref: But more than anything else, put God's work first and do what he wants. Then the other things will be yours as well.
text: Kafɔɔ bita anu asɛɛ ye di Yaa Kafa Sɛka ɔkpɛ suoto alenkee di asaa amuu ɔbla kamɛ, biɛbla nwɔ dibiesaa. Ni Yaa mayɔ asaa amuu bule ankyakaa ye.
translated: The church members should go to God's House on Sunday to worship God with joy, not with sadness. Because God does not like sadness.

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Binyu bakansiɛ ba nikɔflɛlɛtɛ mankyi ni, mánkpɛ kofe, mánfee alesaa kafɔɔ bakpee di akpa. Kafɔɔ Teeye wɔ ninkpe ni di osi konyu ma obe lele. Biefũ kɔya bilenkee bakansiɛ?
translated: When people want to catch monkeys, they put coffee, they put salt in a small gourd and tie it to a tree. The small gourd has a hole in it just big enough for a hand to enter. Why do they do this to catch people?

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: Kafɔɔ kɔtɔkɔ ye nlɛ, bia Ɔka Salomo omu, di lɛsaawɛ nɔɔ nimuu kamɛ, atakpee awu alɛ onyu aawo lepoo kamɛ atoto kpeele ya mmle kaamɛ lɛma onwii.
translated: When the Queen of Sheba heard about Solomon's fame, she came to test him with difficult questions, and she arrived with a very large caravan with camels carrying spices and gold and precious stones.

ref: Don't condemn others, and God won't condemn you.
text: “Bitankanko batii, alɛ Yaa kafɔɔ dilaakanko ye.
translated: The child is crying, but Yaa's child is not crying.

ref: Treat others as you want them to treat you. This is what the Law and the Prophets are all about. The Narrow Gate (Luke
text: Asaa ya biomiɛ bilɛ batii bamba babla bata ye ni yi, aye bibla nkpo okle bita ma. Nle mmle ninle Mose kufiofa ku Yaa ɔlaa babuɛtɛtɛ asaa ya bootuo ni kasɔ nɛ. Bife Di Kayoko Ka Ninle Ni Kabiibii Kamɛ Bibuo.
translated: When people do not follow God's laws, they bring problems upon themselves. That is why Moses wrote down God's commandments so that people would know them. The Ten Commandments That We Should All Obey.

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and winds beat against that house. But it was built on solid rock, and so it did not fall.
text: Kanto lanɔɔ, sekle layii, afɛɛfɔ kafɔɔ lafɛ osie kanya di leyo nwu suoto. Kafɔɔ ditabiɛ, diekye baatofo ni di difuɔnkpaa suoto.
translated: In the past, when a woman was pregnant, her husband would not sleep with her until after she gave birth. After giving birth, she would stay with the baby for three years.

ref: He taught them like someone with authority, and not like their teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: Asaa nɔɔ otuo ditate fɛ mmle okle di kufiofa batuotɛ ntuo nii. Kafɔɔ aatuo asaa ku sɛkambi.
translated: The man is standing at the door because he wants to greet the chief. So the man is waiting outside.

ref: Jesus put his hand on the man and said, “I want to! Now you are well.” At once the man's leprosy disappeared.
text: Yesu ladiki kɔnɛɛ aakpanko nwɔ, aatɔkɔ nwɔ alɛ, “Letuna, suoto lɛfɔ siklekle!” Nfanwu, ofiɛ nwu layɔɔ osuɔtɔ nwu nkpo.
translated: Jesus took the girl by the hand and said to her, "Little girl, get up!" Immediately, she got up and began to walk.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “He healed our diseases and made us well.” Some Who Wanted To Go with Jesus (Luke
text: Aabla nle mmle alɛ, ɔlaa wɔ di Yaa Ɔlaa Buɛtɛtɛ Yesaia labuɛ aatɛ ni kawa kaanya. Alɛ, “Nnwɔɔ omu nɔɔ eefũ wo suoto dibuo, eekpee disi kafɔɔ di sifiɛ loo simuu kalɔ.” Batii Banyɔ Lamiɛ Alɛ Bootootikanko Yesu.
translated: Long ago, the Great Prophet Isaiah wrote about this matter. He said, "A voice of someone is crying out in the wilderness, prepare the way of the Lord, make his paths straight." John the Baptist Was Preparing the Way for Jesus.

ref: so the disciples went over to him and woke him up. They said, “Lord, wake up! Save us before we drown!”
text: Basaateketetɛ nwu nkpo lakyɛ baalaasɛnkɛsa nwɔ, ni baatɔkɔ nwɔ alɛ, “Saate, diki wo! Nfɛ buookpi lo!”
translated: They brought him something wrapped in a cloth, and they said to him, "Teacher, look at this! It's a very big fish!"

ref: The men in the boat were amazed and said, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him.” Two Men with Demons in Them (Mark
text: Diabla obiala lɛma yaaa. Ni baakaalɛ suoto lɛma alɛ, “Ɔmɛntii okle ninle nwɔ mmle di kɔfɛɛfɔ ku ntu be ninkɔkpɛ ni nnu nwɔ ɔlaa?” Yesu Latososa Anwuna Kpile Di Batii Banyɔ Bawo Kamɛ.
translated: The devil left him for a time. And the angels came and said, "Why are you looking for the living among the dead? Jesus Has Risen From the Dead Just As He Said."

ref: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.

translated: The bird flew over the house and landed on the tree.

ref: The people taking care of the pigs ran to the town and told everything, especially what had happened to the two men.
text: Nioso aplakuo nwu bakpatɛ latoso baasifi okpoo baalaabuɛ lɛsaa le niawa ni di basuɔtɔ ba kamɛ di anwuna kpile nwu lasiɛ ni ku aplakuo nwu suoto baatɔkɔ batii.
translated: The church should not only preach the gospel to people so they can become believers, but it should also teach them how to live according to God's word so the church can grow and become strong.

ref: Jesus got into a boat and crossed back over to the town where he lived.
text: Yesu labuo di oklo nwu nkpo kamɛ aakple aataalɛ lekpo nwu nkpo aasifi okpoo nɔɔ.
translated: Jesus said that they should give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar and give to God what belongs to God.

ref: Go and learn what the Scriptures mean when they say, ‘Instead of offering sacrifices to me, I want you to be merciful to others.’ I didn't come to invite good people to be my followers. I came to invite sinners.” People Ask about Going without Eating (Mark
text: Bitɔɔkyɛ biɛlaawolaa lɛsaa le di Kɔkpana Klekle Kukũ kamɛ kotuo ni alɛ, ‘Sifutulɛ di Yaa komiɛ, diele babɔkɛɛ ɔyɔ ɔta ole.’ Ninwa babienetɛ ɔlɛɛ, bakpiletɛ oso lɛwa.” Baakaalɛ Yesu Sɛlaa Di Kanya Onii Suoto.
translated: The disciples who went to the Upper Room said, 'Truly the Lord has risen, and has appeared to Simon.' Then they also told what had happened on the road." They Called Jesus the Son of God Who Rose.

ref: But they left and talked about him to everyone in that part of the country. Jesus Heals a Man Who Could Not Talk
text: Kafɔɔ baadie ni ninfa yi, baasifi baalaayie ɔlaa wɔ mmle lɛkya di Yesu suoto baakyakasa ntɛɛ nwu nkpo kasɔ fɛɛ. Yesu Layɔɔsa Osuɔtɔ Onwii Nintatoofuo Ni Ɔkakatɛ Ofiɛ.
translated: After his death and resurrection, his disciples began to preach about Jesus and many people believed. Jesus Christ the Son of God Saves Us From Our Sins.

ref: But when someone arrests you, don't worry about what you will say or how you will say it. At that time you will be given the words to say.
text: Nse bɛɛkpaa ye bɛɛkyɛnko kakankɔ yi, bitambu diisi di ɔlaa wɔ bibabuɛ ni suoto, ku mmle bibabuɛ nwu nii. Nse obe lewo alɛ biakakatɛ yi, Yaa omu mata ye ɔlaa wɔ biabuɛ nii.
translated: When a person becomes a Christian, they must put away old things and forget them, and not return to old things again. When someone does this, God will give them new things.

ref: But you will not really be the one speaking. The Spirit from your Father will tell you what to say.
text: Diekye diele aye bamu nimakakatɛ. Teeye wɔ ninle ni Yaa Ninwuna nimakakatɛ ninfenko di suoto lee.
translated: We should not be afraid of people. Because we are in God's hands and nothing bad will happen to us.

ref: When people mistreat you in one town, hurry to another one. I promise you before you have gone to all the towns of Israel, the Son of Man will come.
text: Nse beetikanko ye di okpoo onwii kamɛ yi, bietoso bisifi ɔbamba kamɛ. Nwaako kɔtɔkɔ ye nlɛ bilabaloo sɛkpɛ lee ɔbla di Israel sekpoo simuu kamɛ fiɛ di ami, Otii Obi nwu, makple anwa.
translated: When we were still in our father's house, we used to hear many stories. One of the stories that we heard most often was about the time when the children of Israel left Egypt to go to their own land, the Promised Land.

ref: Students are not better than their teacher, and slaves are not better than their master.
text: “Saateketetɛ ninlenkee saatuotɛ nɔɔ, nkpo kafɔɔ di ɔlanle ninlenkee saate nɔɔ nɛ.
translated: When you are going to do something, first think about how you will do that thing.

ref: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
text: Kaplɛ kanwii kafuo bia kutĩitĩi kɔnyɔ ɔya. Kafɔɔ kukanwii ninkpa kaapɛ kaasɔ alɛ, Teeye Yaa dieye nwu ole.
translated: The child's mother is coming from the farm today. When the child's mother arrives, we will eat food, God willing.

ref: If you tell others you belong to me, I will tell my Father in heaven you are my followers.
text: “Otii lele niediki eebuɛ di batii anu alɛ, ole mi ale yi, amii madiki nwɔ kanya di Teemi anu di Yaa kafa nlɛ ole mi ale.
translated: When you do not do what is good in your life, you will not be able to enter the Kingdom of God and live there forever.

ref: But if you reject me, I will tell my Father in heaven you don't belong to me. Not Peace, but Trouble (Luke
text: Kafɔɔ nse otii owo lesĩ mi di batii anu alɛ aaye mi yi, ami, masĩ nwɔ di Teemi anu di osi nlɛ ninye nwɔ. Batii Masɛ Suoto Ta Ami Oso.
translated: When someone does something bad to you, don't do bad things back to them, but instead do good things to the person who did bad to you. Because God Loves You Too.

ref: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
text: “Bitanyu bilɛ atoko ɔkyɛ ole lɛyɔ lɛwako kanyi ka mmle. Oowo! Ninwako atoko ɔkyɛ ole. Kafɔɔ kapamii lɛwako.
translated: The child is eating food that his mother cooked for him. Wow! The child is eating food. The food is very delicious.

ref: If you love your father or mother or even your sons and daughters more than me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
text: “Otii lele niɛbɔmbɔ ote ee ɔya eelenkee mi yi, alaafuo mi saateketetɛ ole. Olele kafɔɔ niɛbɔmbɔ obi suɔtɔbi ee obi sankobi eelenkee mi yi, aasiɛko alɛ aale mi saateketetɛ.
translated: When you receive advice that is good for you, accept it with humility. When someone gives you counsel or corrects you for your good, thank them and accept it with humility.

ref: And anyone who gives one of my most humble followers a cup of cool water, just because that person is my follower, will be rewarded.
text: Bitofo nle mmle nwaa bilɛ, otii lele niekusii ntu yɔɔle kakɔkɔɛ kanwii ɛɛta batikankotɛ nii ba mmle ɔyɛntɛle kaamɛ lɛma ta ami oso, Yaa dilabasĩ nwɔ letota biene ɔta.”
translated: When you go to the farm, you should wear long-sleeved clothes and long trousers so that the mosquitoes will not bite you and give you malaria, because there is no hospital nearby.

ref: As John's followers were going away, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What sort of person did you go out into the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
text: Di obe wɔ di basaateketetɛ nwu nkpo nfɛ kosifi nii yi, Yesu lakakatɛ di Yohane suoto aatɔkɔ batii dikudi nwu nkpo alɛ, “Obe wɔ biadie biakyɛ Yohane nfũ di ɔfaafuu kamɛ, be biatoonyu osuku bilɛ bianya? Lɛkpɛnyi le di kɔfɛɛfɔ latɔɔfɛ ni?
translated: When they had gone, Jesus began to speak to the crowds about John, saying, "What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken by the wind?"

ref: to ask Jesus, “Are you the one we should be looking for? Or must we wait for someone else?”
text: Ni balaakaalɛ nwɔ alɛ, “Afɔ ninle otii wɔ di Yaa labuɛ aatɛ alɛ fabawa ni ɛɛ, sɛɛ buatoonyu osuku munta otii bamba?”
translated: They asked him, "You are not also one of this man's disciples, are you, for you deny it three times?"

ref: People of Capernaum, do you think you will be honored in heaven? You will go down to hell! If the miracles that took place in your town had happened in Sodom, it would still be standing.
text: Ni aye Kapernaum batii, bienyu bilɛ Yaa matakatɛsa ye asifiko osi? Oowo! Abawakosa ye kasɔ ankpee ye di kawɔɔ ɔtɔ kamɛ. Diekye sɛkpɛ se niɛbla ni ɔkpɛ bɛɛbla ni kaamɛ lee, nse mambla sĩ di Sodom yi, nse okpoo nwu nte diɛbase bia miɛ.
translated: And you, Capernaum, will you be lifted up to heaven? No! You will go down to Hades. For if the miracles that were performed in you had been performed in Sodom, it would have remained to this day.

ref: At that moment Jesus said: My Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I am grateful that you hid all this from wise and educated people and showed it to ordinary people.
text: Di obe nwu nkpo kamɛ yi, Yesu labuɛ alɛ, “Teete, osi ku kasɔ Saate, kɔpɛ fɔ sɛfa, diekye asaa ya fɛɛyɔ feekookaa ni bakũyentɛ ku banumbentɛ, nya fatoodiki feetuo batii ba ninanye ni akũ nɛ.
translated: Jesus answered them, "Truly, truly, I say to you, you seek me, not because you saw signs, but because you ate your fill of the loaves."

ref: This yoke is easy to bear, and this burden is light.

text: Kafɔɔ nse buobuɛ bulɛ ‘otii nfũ kuokye’ yi, buɔyɛkɛ bulɛ batii ba mmle mabla wo lɛsaa, diekye bamuu lɛma beefũ beenu alɛ Yohane yi, Yaa ɔlaa buɛtɛtɛ aale.”
translated: When they heard the two disciples say 'behold the Lamb of God', they left the one they were following, and the two of them followed Jesus, as John had said.

text: “Ninfɛ di ote nwu nkpo lakyɛ obi nyɔɔfa nfũ, aalaatɔkɔ nwɔ ɔlaa nwu nkpo okle kafɔɔ. Ni aatuna alɛ, ‘Teete lenu, makyɛ.’ Kafɔɔ atakyɛ.

text: Ninfɛ di Yesu layɛntɛ ku lɛkpa le mmle alɛ, “Yaa lɛlɛɛ batii kpi, kafɔɔ, basɛɛ eediki kaamɛ.” Yesu Lakakatɛ Di Akpooto Ɔta Suoto.

text: Ɔblɔ lafiɛ ɔka nwu nkpo osie kanya ni aakyesee bakpɛtɛ nɔɔ baakyɛ baalaalo batii lotɛ nwu nkpo bamuu, ni baakpee okpoo lɛma nwu nkpo ɔtɔ baawɔɔsa nwu nɛ.
translated: He said that he will buy things today because tomorrow the shops will be closed and they will not open, and he needs to get things that they will use at home.

ref: The crowds were surprised to hear what Jesus was teaching. The Most Important Commandment (Mark
text: Di obe wɔ di batii dikudi nwu nkpo lanu ni asaatuo ya mmle yi, diabla ma ɔkpɛ osie kanya. Yesu Latuo Asaa Di Kufiofa Ko Niefĩ Kulenke Ni Suoto.
translated: When you go to the market to buy things on Saturday or Sunday, the devil will catch you. Jesus Wants Us to Rest on His Holy Day and Worship.

ref: ‘The Lord said to my Lord: Sit at my right side until I make your enemies into a footstool for you.’
text: ‘Yaa latɔkɔ Saate nii alɛ, “Siɛ di oletanɛɛkyɛ nii ninfũ dilaase di obe wɔ mayɔ balo lɛfɔ nkpee fɔ ni ninkpaa kalɔ.” ’
translated: Then Satan answered the Lord, "Does Job fear God for nothing? Have you not put a hedge around him and his household and everything he has?"

ref: “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose family will he come from?” They answered, “He will be a son of King David.”

ref: And now your temple will be deserted.

translated: Long ago, when there were no cars, people used to travel on foot.

text: “Mambla lɛsaa biala baatii kanya ma ni fɛle Yaa bakpɛmblatɛ nwaa bale nii. Asaa ya man-yɔ mannii ni disi ku nnɛɛtũ lɛma lɛbla blɛfɛɛ, bɛɛkpana Kɔkpana Klekle sɛlaa beetika nya di suoto. Bɛɛbla kafɔɔ awu sɛkpaalɛ abinte beetika di awu lɛma suoto.
translated: Long ago, people did not know God and they worshipped idols. At that time, people lived in darkness and did not know the truth, because the Devil deceived them and led them astray. They made images with their own hands and worshipped those images.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You lock people out of the kingdom of heaven. You won't go in yourselves, and you keep others from going in.
text: Biyɔɔsa aye kufiofa batuotɛ ku Farisi batii! Aye anu di anu basaablatɛ! Minkyɛ bakpisɔfɔ nfũ bilɛ biɔkyakaako ma, kafɔɔ minkyɔnkyɔ ma minfũ ma asaa ninnɛɛ. Mimpɛ ɔlaa minklɛsa kafɔɔ bilɛ biɔyɔ minkyɔnkyɔ ma. Ta nkpo oso letokonanfi lee matɛ nimpɔ.
translated: Woe to you scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! You are like whitewashed tombs! You clean the outside of the cup and dish, but inside they are full of greed and self-indulgence. translated: Blessed are those who wash their robes! They will have the right to eat from the tree of life, and they will have the right to enter through the gates into the city of God.

translated: Whatever the person who is speaking says, and if God's word is in it, then God will be in the person's mouth and will speak through the person.

translated: "In the past, some people used to think that the earth was flat and that if you traveled far enough, you would fall off the edge."

translated: When Jesus and his disciples arrived at the garden called Gethsemane. He told them, "Sit here while I go and pray to God."

text: Yesu lakple aayila aatɔɔkakatɛ ko ma fiɛ di Yuda wɔ ninle ni lefosi batii banyɔ nwu nkpo onwii lɛma lawa. Batii dikudi le di olebatatɛ banɔɔfo ku kufiofa batuotɛ ku okpoo banɔɔfo ladiki ni latikanko nwɔ baatɔɔwa. Baaklee nwosoku ku mpamii.
translated: Jesus and his disciples were going through the grain fields on the Sabbath, and his disciples began to pick some heads of grain. The Pharisees said to him, "Look, why are they doing what is unlawful on the Sabbath?" They were hungry and ate.

translated: When Judas, the betrayer of Jesus, saw that Jesus had been condemned, he repented and returned the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders, saying, "I have sinned by betraying innocent blood." But they said, "What is that to us? See to it yourself."

ref: One of Jesus' followers pulled out a sword. He struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
text: Ni baatii ba niawɛ ni di Yesu ɔkyɛ, onwii lɛma ladiki kapamii nɔɔ di kotofo kamɛ aasafɔ Oletatɛ Nɔɔfo kpɛmblatɛ kotoko aakpa nɛ.
translated: Those who believe in Jesus and follow his word will receive eternal life through the Holy Spirit who lives in their hearts.

ref: The high priest then tore his robe and said, “This man claims to be God! We don't need any more witnesses! You have heard what he said.
text: Obe wɔ di Oletatɛ Nɔɔfo lanu ni ɔlaa wɔ mmle, ni aabɛ awu nɔɔ aabuɛ alɛ, “Bienu! Ebuɛ mbusuo ɔlaa! Diesinfĩ alɛ otii kuonwii kayila ɔlaa owo kamaa!
translated: When you become a follower of the Lord Jesus and you die, people will say, "Good! That person has gone to rest! Because now that person is in a good place!"

ref: But Jesus did not answer. So the high priest said, “With the living God looking on, you must tell the truth. Are you the Messiah, the Son of God?”
text: Kafɔɔ Yesu lamaasa kanya, atabuɛ ɔlaa kuonwii. Ninfɛ di Oletatɛ Nɔɔfo lakple aatɔkɔ nwɔ alɛ, “Lɛka Yaa wɔ ninkpe ni nkpa lekpete fɔ nlɛ tɔkɔwo, nse afɔ ninle Kristo nwu nkpo, Yaa wɔ ninkpe ni nkpa Obi.”
translated: Before Jesus came to earth, He was in heaven. Then God the Holy Spirit spoke to the people saying, "If you believe and obey my words, then when Christ comes, you will believe and obey Him."

ref: “That is what you say!” Jesus answered. “But I tell all of you, ‘Soon you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right side of God All-Powerful and coming on the clouds of heaven.’ ”
text: Ninfɛ di Yesu ladiki kanya alɛ, “Ɛɛ! Nwu fatoobuɛ fɛ mmle kute ni nɛ. Kafɔɔ kɔtɔkɔ ye nlɛ, diɛyɔ di nua diesifi nii, bibanya Otii Obi nwu nkpo nsi di Yaa wɔ ninlenke ni oletanɛɛkyɛ. Bibanyɔɔ kafɔɔ eekye osi ɔɔwa di alokonkyɛ kamɛ!”
translated: Then Jesus' disciples said to him, "Yes! Now you are speaking plainly to us. Because you have spoken clearly, we understand what you are saying, and we believe that God has sent you, and you know everything without anyone having to tell you. Because of that, we believe that you came from God!"

ref: Then they spit in his face and hit him with their fists. Others slapped him
text: Ninfɛ baatufa sita baakpete nwɔ di anu, ni baapɛ nwɔ nɛ. Bawo kafɔɔ lapɛ nwɔ nnɛɛ di atokosi.
translated: Six women came to the river to fetch water, and they fetched water. They returned and gave water to their husbands.

ref: and Peter remembered that Jesus had said, “Before a rooster crows, you will say three times you don't know me.” Then Peter went out and cried bitterly.
text: Ninfɛ di Petro lanyuma alɛ Yesu latɔkɔ nwɔ alɛ, “Fiɛ di kɔɔkɔ mabo nii, fabasĩ mi sitiɛ fambuɛ falɛ, faaye mi.” Nkpo oso aadie aasifi kayi aalaawi kaku osie kanya.
translated: Peter's wife's mother was lying down with fever, and they told Jesus about her, saying, "Our father's child is very sick, please come and see her, help her." When Jesus touched her hand, the fever left her and she got up.

ref: Then they had a meeting and decided to buy a field that belonged to someone who made clay pots. They wanted to use it as a graveyard for foreigners.
text: Di obe wɔ baakpee ni asi baawo, baatuna alɛ babayɔ koto nwu nkpo man-ya dibukii blatɛ kasɔ, nfa kafɔɔ babatookookaa bafɔɔ nɛ.
translated: When they reached the place where they were going, they found that the people had already prepared food and drinks, so they sat down and ate together.

ref: This is why people still call that place “Field of Blood.”
text: Nle mmle oso, bia miɛ bɔɔlɛɛ kasɔ nwu nkpo alɛ Ntɔ Kasɔ nɛ.
translated: When you come tomorrow, bring me the book that is in the Big House.

ref: Jesus was brought before Pilate the governor, who asked him, “Are you the king of the Jews?” “Those are your words!” Jesus answered.
text: Baakpaa Yesu baalaase di Roma batii abaa ɔnɔɔfo Pilato anu. Ni aakaalɛ nwɔ alɛ, “Afɔ ninle Yuda batii Ɔka nwu nɛ?” Ninfɛ di Yesu ladiki kanya alɛ, “Nnwu fatoobuɛ nɛ.”
translated: Then they took Jesus to the Roman governor Pilate. And they began to accuse him, saying, "This man claims to be King of the Jews." So Jesus answered him briefly, saying, "You have said it yourself."

ref: But Jesus did not say anything, and the governor was greatly amazed. The Death Sentence (Mark
text: Kafɔɔ Yesu lasĩ atabɛ kanya adiki ɔlaa kuonwii kanya. Nioso diabla abaa nɔɔfo nwu nkpo ɔkpɛ. Pilato Lalo Yesu Kukpi Lɛpɔɔ.
translated: When Jesus came down from the mountain, large crowds followed him. Then the devil left him for a time. Pilate Handed Jesus Over to be Crucified.

ref: Pilate saw that there was nothing he could do and that the people were starting to riot. So he took some water and washed his hands in front of them and said, “I won't have anything to do with killing this man. You are the ones doing it!”
text: Di obe wɔ di Pilato lanya alɛ ɔlɔbasinfuo ni lele kuniwii ɔbla, kafɔɔ kudu nfɛ nimadie ni yi, ni aayɔ ntu, aafoto nnɛɛ nɔɔ di batii nwu nkpo dikudi anu, aabuɛ alɛ, “Nnɛɛ nii diesi di osuɔtɔ wɔ mmle kukpi kamɛ lo! Oleye kule lo!”
translated: When they brought him to Pilate's court and laid out the charges against him, and after much discussion and examination, and having found no fault worthy of death or imprisonment in him, he said, "I find no basis for any charge against this man! Release him!"

ref: There they gave Jesus some wine mixed with a drug to ease the pain. But when Jesus tasted what it was, he refused to drink it.
text: Ninfɛ baayɔ nta be bɛɛkpɔtɔɔsa ni ku kɔfa lonle baata Yesu alɛ anyi nɛ. Kafɔɔ obe wɔ aatu aanyu nii, aasĩ mi onyi.
translated: Many people did not believe that Jesus was the Son of God. Because they did not see him with their own eyes, they did not know him.

ref: shouted, “So you're the one who claimed you could tear down the temple and build it again in three days! If you are God's Son, save yourself and come down from the cross!”
text: “Afɔ niabuɛ falɛ fababiɛ Yaa Olekatakɔ ka mmle, fanse ni letofo di ayi atiɛ kamɛ nɛ! Nse Yaa Obi fale nwaako, faasoo koowoso nwu fawa, faafũ suoto lɛfɔ nkpa buonyu!”
translated: The third leg that supports Yaa Olekatakɔ's stool has broken, so the chief cannot sit on it anymore! If Yaa Obi had been there, they would have woven strong rope, they would have carved a good stool leg to replace it!

ref: One of them at once ran and grabbed a sponge. He soaked it in wine, then put it on a stick and held it up to Jesus.
text: Nfanwu onwii kaamɛ lɛma latoso aalaayɔ oleteku aanyɔ di nta nyankale kamɛ, aayɔ aatũ di owoso suoto, aamiɛ alɛ ɔɔyɔ ankyɔɔ nwɔ annyi.
translated: When someone is going to do something and they are not sure whether they will succeed or not, they say this proverb to encourage themselves.

ref: Many women had come with Jesus from Galilee to be of help to him, and they were there, looking on at a distance.
text: Basanko ba kpinwu kafɔɔ niatikanko Yesu baatɔɔkyakaako nwɔ ni, baakye ni Galilea lawɛ ninfa. Baayila kaakyonkɔ baatoonyu asaa ya niatɔɔwa nii.
translated: After Jesus had finished instructing his twelve disciples, he left that place and went to teach and preach in the towns of Galilee.

ref: That evening a rich disciple named Joseph from the town of Arimathea
text: Obe wɔ kɔɔtɔɔfɔ lawo nii, osuɔtɔ onwii niale ni saawɛntɛ baatɔɔlɛɛ ni Yosef lawa, aakye Arimatea. Yesu saateketetɛ aale.
translated: There was a rich man, whose name was Joseph from Arimathea. He was a disciple of Jesus.

ref: All this time Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were sitting across from the tomb.
text: Ninfɛ di Maria Magdalene ku Maria nyɔɔfa lasiɛ ninfa baakyɔɔ ɔkya nwu nkpo anu nɛ. Basaanyuntɛ Layila Baatoonyu Yesu Ɔkya Nwu Nkpo.
translated: The women Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went early in the morning to see the tomb. They found the Angel of the Lord had rolled away the stone from Jesus' tomb.

ref: So please order the tomb to be carefully guarded for three days. If you don't, his disciples may come and steal his body. They will tell the people he has been raised to life, and this last lie will be worse than the first one.”
text: Nkpo oso ta falɛ basaanyuntɛ lɛlaayila beenyu ɔkya nwu nkpo osie kanya diɛlaase di ayi atiɛ kamɛ. Nse faata babla nle mmle yi, basaateketetɛ nɔɔ makyɛ manayu nwɔ mansifiko, manatɔkɔ batii alɛ, ‘Atɔɔtaka eekye bakpi kamɛ.’ Nse diɛwa ninkpo yi, sɛlaa kuafunu se babakple malaa ni malenkee lɛkasale.”
translated: One thing that really troubles many people today is that they don't know what will happen tomorrow. When they think about the future, they become worried about their children, their grandchildren and they say, 'What will become of them?' When such things happen, it is good that we remember our God and his promises.

ref: Go to the people of all nations and make them my disciples. Baptize them in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit,
text: Nkpo oso bitɔɔkyɛ kayi ka mmle nnle mmuu kamɛ, biɛlaabla batii basaateketetɛ nii. Biekpeesa ma Yaa ntu di Ote, Obi, ku Ninwuna Klekle leyooto kamɛ.
translated: They did not accept the message that was being preached to them, so they continued to live in sin. They refused to worship the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit alone.

ref: He said, “The time has come! God's kingdom will soon be here. Turn back to God and believe the good news!” Jesus Chooses Four Fishermen (Matthew
text: Aatoobuɛ alɛ, “Obe biene nwu ntoowo, Yaa sɛka ole bee ntɔɔtɛɛtɛɛ, bidiɛ sikpile ɔbla biefũ ɔlaa biene nwu binu!”
translated: The bus said, "You have done well, God will give you long life, because you helped me when I was in trouble!"

ref: At once Jesus asked them to come with him. They left their father in the boat with the hired workers and went with him. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Luke
text: Di Yesu lanya ma nii, ni aalɛɛ ma alɛ batikanko nwɔ nɛ. Ni baadiɛ ote lɛma Sebedeo ku bakpɛntɛ nɔɔ di oklo kamɛ baatikanko nwɔ nɛ. Yesu Latososa Ninwuna Kpile Di Osuɔtɔ Onwii Kamɛ.
translated: When Jesus saw them, he called his two disciples. And they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired servants and followed him. Jesus Chooses Four Fishermen as His Disciples.

ref: Jesus went to her. He took hold of her hand and helped her up. The fever left her, and she served them a meal.
text: Nioso Yesu lakyɛ ɔkyɛ nɔɔ aalaamufũ nwɔ kɔɔnɛɛ aatakatɛsa, ni di ofiɛ layɔɔ nwɔ nɛ, ni aataka aanyu ma kanya nɛ.
translated: When Jesus had finished giving these instructions to his twelve disciples, he departed from there to teach and preach in their towns.

ref: Suddenly a man with an evil spirit in him entered the synagogue and yelled,
text: Nfanwu osuɔtɔ onwii di ninwuna kpile lasiɛ nii lawɛ kaamɛ lɛma,
translated: The child who does not listen to advice will follow the corpse to the grave,

ref: Jesus told the evil spirit, “Be quiet and come out of the man!”
text: Ni di Yesu lamuɛ nwɔ alɛ, “Lo kanana, faadie nwɔ kaamɛ!”
translated: "When Jesus saw her, he said, "Woman, you are freed from your infirmity!""

ref: Simon and the others started looking for him.
text: Sɛmaa Simon ku bawo nɔɔ lakyɛ baalaatowolaa Yesu.
translated: Then Simon and those with him searched for Jesus.

ref: when four people came up, carrying a man on a mat because he could not walk.
text: Ni baatii bana latuka fiɛtɛ onwii niafefee nii baatɔɔwako ɔkyɛ nɔɔ nɛ.
translated: The child who was playing outside has now come inside the house.

ref: Once again, Jesus went to the shore of Lake Galilee. A large crowd gathered around him, and he taught them.
text: Ni di Yesu lakple aadie ninfa aasifi Galilea lekpo kotoko ni baatii kpinwu lawa nwɔ ɔkyɛ ninfa ni aatuo ma asaa nɛ.
translated: When Jesus and his disciples returned to Galilee, they went to a house and he began to teach them again.

ref: One Sabbath Jesus and his disciples were walking through some wheat fields. His disciples were picking grains of wheat as they went along.
text: Di Lɛnyɛɛtɛyi, Yesu ku basaateketetɛ nɔɔ latoofe di atita kofe kunwii kamɛ. Ni di basaateketetɛ nɔɔ, lafee atita nwu kɔɔne awo baatoowee nɛ.
translated: At the Last Supper, Jesus and his disciples ate the Passover meal together. During the meal with his disciples, he gave thanks for the bread and wine and shared it with them.

ref: He even had to tell his disciples to get a boat ready to keep him from being crushed by the crowds.
text: Ta batii dikudi nwu lɛpɔ oso Yesu latɔkɔ basaateketetɛ nɔɔ alɛ bamiɛ oklo owo batɔɔ aasiɛ kaamɛ mmle baatii dikudi nwu dilaba pimpi nwɔ ni.
translated: And they brought little children to Jesus so that he might touch them, but the disciples rebuked those who were bringing the little children.

ref: There were also James and John, the two sons of Zebedee. Jesus called them Boanerges, which means “Thunderbolts.”
text: ku Yakobo ku obilɛma suɔtɔbi Yohane mba ninle ni Sebedeo babi ba di Yesu lata ma ni leyoto alɛ Boanege, lɛɛsɔ diki nɔɔ ninle ni ɔplalaa.
translated: And James and his brother John, the sons of Zebedee, Jesus gave them the name Boanerges, which means sons of thunder.

ref: A nation whose people fight each other won't last very long.
text: Nse sɛka kalekɔ kawo lɛsɛ kɛtaka keeyila di suoto nɔɔ, ni sɛka kalekɔ nwu dilabafuo oyila.
translated: If you want to learn something well, you must practice it every day, and you must not be lazy about it.

ref: And a family that fights won't last long either.
text: Nkpo kafɔɔ sɛ leyo lewo lɛsɛ di akyuu anyɔ bɔɔkpɛko bawo ni leyo nwu dilabafuo oyila.
translated: Things that are good are those that we do to help others and not those that we do to show off.

ref: Jesus said this because the people were saying that he had an evil spirit in him. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Matthew
text: Yesu labuɛ sɛlaa se mmle, diekye baatoobuɛ alɛ, ninwuna kpile nsi nnwɔɔ Yesu. Bawe Ninle Yesu Babilɛma Sankobi Ku Babilɛma Suɔtɔbi.
translated: When Jesus was born, wise men came from the east, following a star to find Jesus. They Brought Jesus Gold, Frankincense and Myrrh.

ref: Then he looked at the people sitting around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.
text: Ni aanyu batii ba ninsi bɛɛmana nwɔ beekyi ni, ni aabuɛ alɛ, “Binyu, yaami ku babiloo ninle mba ninsi ni ninfũ nɛ!
translated: When you see that these things are beginning to happen, you should say, "Truly, the time has come when these things will be fulfilled!

ref: Finally, Jesus said: What is God's kingdom like? What story can I use to explain it?
text: Yesu lakple aabuɛ alɛ, “Be suoto kayɔ Yaa sɛka kalekɔ nkaatɛɛsa? Lɛmɛnkpa kayɔ ndiki ni kasɔ?
translated: "Jesus answered them, "Do you think that these Galileans were worse sinners than all the other Galileans because they suffered this way?"

ref: Listen carefully to what you hear! The way you treat others will be the way you will be treated—and even worse.
text: Yesu lakple aatɔkɔ ma alɛ, “Bikyɔɔ atoko nwaa di sɛlaa se bionu ni suoto! Diekye leklapoosaa le biɛyɔ biɛklapoosa biɛta ni bawo lee, nninwu ke di Yaa mayɔ anklapoosa anta ye nɛ, bia lele ye malenkee.
translated: Jesus answered them, "Truly I say to you, you seek me not because you saw signs, but because you ate the loaves and were filled. Do not work for the food that perishes, but for the food that endures to eternal life."

ref: Again Jesus said: God's kingdom is like what happens when a farmer scatters seed in a field.
text: Yesu lakple abuɛ alɛ, “Yaa sɛka kalekɔ nte fɛ mmle di otii nsa ni kamɔɔ kubi koofe.
translated: Jesus answered them, "Truly I tell you, you seek me not because you saw signs but because you ate the loaves and were filled."

ref: It is the ground that makes the seeds sprout and grow into plants that produce grain.
text: Kasɔ kamu ninta kamɔɔ kubi nwu mmuɔ kantika abi lɛnya. Lɛkasale kankɔntɔ, afata ndie. Sɛmaa kanlaka amɛ kandie kufuo, nni sɛmaa kansiɛ abi kanyi ntu kambe.
translated: When you want to do something good, you must plan well. If you don't plan, it won't work. So before you start any work, first you must think about how you will do it.

ref: That evening, Jesus said to his disciples, “Let's cross to the east side.”
text: Diɛ dii nwu nkpo kɔtɔɔfɔ, Yesu latɔkɔ basaateketetɛ nɔɔ alɛ, “Bita butaalɛ busifi lekpo le mmle sɛmaa.”
translated: After Jesus had finished all these sayings, he said to his disciples, "You know that after two days the Passover is coming."

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed Lake Galilee and came to shore near the town of Gerasa.
text: Nni sɛmaa, Yesu ku basaateketetɛ nɔɔ lataalɛ baalaadu Galilea lekpo nwu kakyɛ nyɔɔfa di Gadara batii kasɔ.
translated: After that, Jesus and his disciples crossed the Sea of Galilee and arrived at the region of the Gadarenes.

ref: He shouted, “Jesus, Son of God in heaven, what do you want with me? Promise me in God's name that you won't torture me!”
text: Ni aayiisa kudu osie kanya alɛ, “Be ɔlaa ninsi di awo ku afɔ ntɛɛ, Yesu, Yaa wɔ ninlenke ni obi! Yaa oso, kotikiti fɔ lekoto, tannanfi mi kotoko!”
translated: "When they saw him, they said, "If you are the Son of God, come down from the cross, Jesus, and save yourself and us! If you do this, we will believe in you, and we will follow you!"

ref: Over on the hillside a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Di obe nwu kamɛ aplakuo ɔlambu kplɛ onwii latoole asaa di kabokotee ɔlɔkɔ.
translated: The child who was crying has now stopped because his mother gave him food.

ref: Then the people started begging Jesus to leave their part of the country.
text: Nioso batii nwu latikiti Yesu lekoto baatɔkɔ nwɔ alɛ adie ma kaasɔ.
translated: The people who saw Jesus' miracles believed in him and followed him.

ref: At that moment Jesus felt power go out from him. He turned to the crowd and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”
text: Nfanwu, Yesu latofo alɛ osie owo ledie nwɔ di suoto. Aakyi aakalɛ batii dikudi nwu alɛ, “Owe niɛkpanko mi awu?”
translated: Then, Jesus asked the woman who had touched him, "Who touched me?" After he had asked this question, he said, "Someone touched me."

ref: They went home with Jairus and saw the people crying and making a lot of noise.
text: Obe wɔ baawa ni Yairo leyo Yesu lanya alɛ batii lesi ninfa boowi kaku ku kudu osie kanya.
translated: They were still on the way to Jairus's house when someone came from his house with the news that his daughter had died.

ref: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, “Talitha, koum!” which means, “Little girl, get up!” The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
text: Nfanwu ɔsankobi nwu lataka aakyɛ. Lefosi alɛɛ anyɔ obi aale. Diatɛ diabla batii nwu ɔkpɛ osie kanya.
translated: The bird flew away this morning. The dog did not bite anyone. The children are playing with their new ball.

ref: But Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened. Then he said, “Give her something to eat.”
text: Yesu lakpee ma kufiofa osie kanya alɛ batanta otii kuonwii lanu, ninfɛ aatɔkɔ ma alɛ, bamiɛ lelesaa batɔɔ ale.
translated: Jesus said that whoever believes in him will not walk in darkness, but will have the light of life.

ref: But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.”
text: Yesu latɔkɔ ma alɛ, “Batii mbu Yaa Ɔlaa buɛtɛtɛ di kakɔ biala diediki okpoo lɛma, kafaabi nɔɔ ku babi lɛma kamɛ manambu nwɔ.”
translated: "Jesus answered them, "My Father is always working, and I also must work.""

ref: He told them, “You may take along a walking stick. But don't carry food or a traveling bag or any money.
text: Ni aakpee ma kufiofa alɛ, di osuku lɛma ɔkyɛ kamɛ batanklee lɛsalɛsaa diediki ɔkyɛnwoso, batanklee alesaa, kotofo ko mankaalɛ ni asaa, batanklee koto.
translated: When we look at our culture, we see that our ancestors left us many things like festivals, traditional dances, drumming and other customs, traditional clothing.

ref: If any place won't welcome you or listen to your message, leave and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.”
text: Nse biɛɛkyɛ okpoo wɔ baatii diefũ ye nii, ee baanu ye nii ɔlaa, biesifi bidiɛ ma, kafɔɔ biɛfɛntɛ nkpaasɔ lee kɔtɛ bitɛ ma. Nni nimatuo alɛ baasĩ ye ofũ nɛ.”
translated: When a child is born into the world, whether it is a boy or a girl, they give them food, so that they will grow strong and become a person. That is how they raise the child until it grows up.

ref: Herod was very sorry for what he had said. But he did not want to break the promise he had made in front of his guests.
text: Ɔlaa nwu labuo Ɔka Herodes suoto osie kanya, kafɔɔ diɛ ntam be aaka ni ku batii ba aakyakako ni oso, aata miɛ alɛ ɔwaalɛ ɔlaa wɔ aabuɛ aatɛ nii.
translated: When King Herod heard this, he was troubled, and he sent and killed all the male children who were two years old and under, according to the time which he had learned from the wise men.

ref: But so many people were coming and going that Jesus and the apostles did not even have a chance to eat. Then Jesus said, “Let's go to a place where we can be alone and get some rest.”
text: Nle mmle sɛmaa, batii kpinwu latɔɔwa ɔkyɛ lɛma diata alɛ batawɛ osuku bale alesaa, nioso Yesu latɔkɔ ma alɛ, “Biwa buɔlatɛ ɔnyɛɛ kɛɛkɛɛ.”
translated: When evening came, the disciples came to him saying that the place was deserted and the time was late, so Jesus should send them away, "Send the people away so they can go buy food."

ref: Then Jesus said, “How much bread do you have? Go and see!” They found out and answered, “We have five small loaves of bread and two fish.”
text: Ni di Yesu latɔkɔ ma alɛ, “Bitɔɔkyɛ biɛlaanyu bilɛ bloblo akudi asɛ ninte ye?” Baakple baabatɔkɔ nwɔ alɛ, “Bloblo akudi anɔɔ ku kɔkpaku sɛnle kɔnyɔ ko kule.”
translated: When Jesus saw them, he said, "Why are you troubled and why do doubts arise in your hearts?" They answered him saying, "We have only five loaves of bread and two fish."

ref: Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples to give to the people. He also divided the two fish, so everyone could have some.
text: Yesu layɔ bloblo akudi anɔɔ ku kɔkpaku nwu kɔnyɔ, ni aanyu osi aakusɛkusɛsa nya. Aabɛbɛɛ bloblo nwu aata basaateketetɛ nɔɔ alɛ basɛ bata batii nwu, ni aabɛbɛɛ kɔkpaku nwu kɔnyɔ kafɔɔ aata batii nwu bamuu nɛ.
translated: Jesus told parables and spoke in proverbs, so that those who listened carefully would understand. He told parables so that those who wanted to learn would come and ask questions, and he spoke in proverbs so that those who asked questions would know the truth.

ref: After everyone had eaten all they wanted,
text: Obiala lɛma lale aamiɛ.
translated: The children went home.

ref: Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
text: Nni kamaa, Yesu omu kafɔɔ lasifi kabokotee osi aalaapɛ ɔlaa aata Yaa.
translated: One day, Jesus went into a boat and crossed to the other side of the Sea.

ref: He then got into the boat with them, and the wind died down. The disciples were completely confused.
text: Ni aayie aabuo di oklo nwu kamɛ di anu lɛma, ni diɛ afɛɛfɔ nwu ladiɛ ɔfɛ nɛ. Diatɛ diabla ma ɔkpɛ osie kanya!
translated: When people gather to do good work together, they should have one mind. That is why we say two heads are better than one!

ref: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
text: Obe wɔ batosoo ni diɛ oklo nwu kamɛ batii lanya Yesu baatɛ nsũ.
translated: They went to the river where John was baptizing people in the name of Jesus.

ref: In every village or farm or marketplace where Jesus went, the people brought their sick to him. They begged him to let them just touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: Kakɔ lele di Yesu lɛkyɛ, nkpayetũ woo, sekpoo woo, ee sefensuku, mankɛnkɛ bafiɛtɛ mamatɛ di asaa kayakɔ aba aba, mantikiti nwɔ lekoto alɛ, ata bafiɛtɛ nwu bakpanko awu nɔɔ kotoko. Batii ba niakpanko nwɔ ni awu kotoko, sifiɛ layɔɔ ma.
translated: When Jesus was born, there was no hospital, no clinic, no medicine, but people lived very long lives, they were very strong, even though they did not have injections or any medicine at all. They did not have injections or medicine at all, yet they were healthy.

ref: The Pharisees and many others obey the teachings of their ancestors. They always wash their hands in the proper way before eating.
text: Baafuo otofo ta Farisi batii ku Yuda batii latɔɔbla bawa lɛma amanle ya baadiɛ baatɛ ma nii. Nioso diediki beefoto nnɛɛ lɛma fɛ mmle baatuo ma nii, fiɛ manle alesaa.
translated: Some of the Pharisees and teachers of the law who had come from Jerusalem gathered around Jesus. They saw some of his disciples eating food with unclean hands, that is, unwashed hands.
